## ⚠️ AMENDMENT 2026-05-05 (post-pivot)

This QA plan was authored against the **initial** Sprint 10 scope. After `/dev-story` Phase 2 discovery on 2026-05-05, Sprint 10 pivoted away from save-persist (deferred to Sprint 11). The following sections of THIS plan are SUPERSEDED:

| Section | Status | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| S10-M1 Save-persist pipeline (`tests/integration/save_load_system/...`) | **REMOVED from Sprint 10** | Deferred to Sprint 11 (story-016 BLOCKED — prerequisites Stories 011 + 012 unimplemented) |
| S10-M4 scene-manager Story 009 (reduce_motion + offline-replay modal) | **REMOVED from Sprint 10** | Depends on save-persist; deferred to Sprint 11 |
| S10-N5 Re-playtest with persisted save | **REMOVED from Sprint 10** | Nothing to playtest until save-persist ships |
| All other rows | **REMAPPED** — see post-pivot story IDs in `sprint-status.yaml` | IDs renumbered: S10-M2/M3 → S10-M1/M2; S10-S1 → S10-M3 (promoted); S10-S3 → S10-M4 (promoted); S10-N1/N2 → S10-S3/S4 (promoted); S10-N3/N4 → S10-N1/N2 |

**Use `production/sprint-status.yaml` as the authoritative story list for Sprint 10 going forward.** A clean re-run of `/qa-plan sprint` is recommended once you're ready to start implementation; this amendment is a stopgap so the plan doesn't silently mislead.

**Tests/evidence rows that REMAIN VALID** (mapped to the post-pivot scope):
- S10-M1 (post-pivot, was M2) — parchment_theme.tres — Visual/Feel + UI manual sign-off
- S10-M2 (post-pivot, was M3) — UIFramework completion — `tests/unit/ui_framework/` apply_parchment_panel + wire_touch_feedback unit tests

- S10-M4 (post-pivot, was S3) — XP feedback — `tests/unit/dungeon_run_orchestrator/xp_grant_on_run_complete_test.gd`

## Automated Tests Required

### S10-M1 Save-persist pipeline end-to-end — Integration

**Test file paths**:
- `tests/integration/save_load_system/full_envelope_round_trip_test.gd` (NEW)
- `tests/integration/save_load_system/heartbeat_persist_cadence_test.gd` (NEW)
- `tests/integration/save_load_system/scene_boundary_persist_trigger_test.gd` (NEW)
- `tests/integration/save_load_system/save_failed_transition_abort_test.gd` (NEW)
- `tests/integration/save_load_system/atomic_write_order_test.gd` (NEW)

**What to test** (mapped to story-016 ACs):

- **AC-1 — Full-state envelope round-trip** (`full_envelope_round_trip_test.gd`):
  - Persist with all 6 consumers populated (Economy gold, HeroRoster + formation, FloorUnlock, FormationAssignment, Recruitment, DungeonRunOrchestrator snapshot if active)
  - File size = `44 + payload_length` bytes (envelope header is fixed 44 bytes per ADR-0004)
  - `MAGIC` field = `0x4C4C474C44` ("LGLD"); `VERSION` field = current schema version
  - HMAC-SHA256 validation succeeds on load
  - Each consumer's `get_save_data()` → `restore_save_data(load_data)` returns identical state
- **AC-2 — Heartbeat persist trigger** (`heartbeat_persist_cadence_test.gd`):
  - Simulate 60s of TickSystem sim-time; assert `request_heartbeat_persist` fires exactly once
  - Heartbeat envelope size ≤ 512 bytes (BLOCKING AC-TICK-11)
  - Heartbeat payload contains ONLY `{t_last_persist, t_session_high_water, sim_tick_counter}` — no full payload
  - `_meta.save_sequence_number` increments by 1 per heartbeat
- **AC-3 — scene_boundary_persist receiver wiring** (`scene_boundary_persist_trigger_test.gd`):
  - Emit `scene_boundary_persist("enter_dungeon_run_view")` from SceneManager BEFORE FADE_TO_BLACK
  - SaveLoadSystem receiver writes full-state envelope on the signal
  - Assert persist completes within 150 ms ± 10 ms (FADE_TO_BLACK budget per AC H-01)
- **AC-4 — save_failed → transition abort** (`save_failed_transition_abort_test.gd`):
  - Mock SaveLoadSystem to emit `save_failed("simulated_disk_full")`
  - Assert SceneManager aborts the in-flight transition (current screen remains active)
  - Assert non-blocking modal appears with "Try Again / Stay Here" cozy copy (per ADR-0007 BLOCKING AC H-07)
- **AC-5 — Atomic write order** (`atomic_write_order_test.gd`):
  - Mock FileAccess to record call sequence; assert order is `.tmp open` → `store_buffer` → `flush` → `DirAccess.rename` → `.bak rotate`
  - Crash-mid-rename simulation: kill process between `.tmp` flush and rename; assert post-recovery file is either valid old `.dat` OR valid new `.dat` (never half-written)
- **AC-6 — No cached consumer refs** (static check, can be a unit test):
  - Reflect on `SaveLoadSystem` instance vars; assert zero vars typed `Node` or any consumer-class
  - All consumer access via `get_node_or_null(path)` per ADR-0003

**Edge cases to cover**:
- Empty payload (all 6 consumers return empty Dictionary): file should still validate
- Payload exceeding mobile budget (synthesize large roster): performance test; should still complete <50 ms p95
- Corrupted MAGIC bytes on load: SaveLoadSystem must fail fast at MAGIC step (validation order: MAGIC → VERSION → HMAC)
- Invalid HMAC: load must fail; error logged; no partial state restoration
- Heartbeat fires during in-flight scene_boundary_persist: serialize correctly (heartbeat may queue or skip per ADR-0005 spec)

**Estimated test count**: ~12 integration tests across 5 files

**Performance tests** (AC-7, AC-8): Add to `tests/performance/save_load_persist_perf_test.gd`:
- 100 successive persist calls with full 6-consumer payload at MVP scale; assert p95 ≤ 10 ms PC
- 100 successive load calls; assert p95 ≤ 50 ms PC
- Mobile budget (50 ms p95 BLOCKING AC-SL-11) validated via Steam Deck smoke separately (no headless mobile target)

### S10-M3 UIFramework completion (apply_parchment_panel + wire_touch_feedback) — Logic + UI

**Test file path**: `tests/unit/ui_framework/apply_parchment_panel_test.gd` (NEW) + `tests/unit/ui_framework/wire_touch_feedback_test.gd` (NEW)

**What to test**:

- **`apply_parchment_panel(panel: Panel, pattern: int)`**:
  - Returns without error on valid Panel input
  - Sets `theme_type_variation` to expected value per pattern enum
  - Idempotent: calling twice on same panel produces identical visual state
  - Defensive: nil panel → push_error + return (no crash)
- **`wire_touch_feedback(control: Control)`**:
  - Connects `gui_input` signal handler (or per-control feedback hook per ADR-0008)
  - On simulated tap: control scales to 1.05× over 80ms (per ADR-0007 §`reduce_motion` exception)
  - On `reduce_motion = true`: feedback still fires (per-control, NOT a transition)
  - Idempotent: calling twice does not duplicate the connection

**Edge cases**:
- Disabled control: feedback does NOT fire on tap
- Control already mid-feedback animation: new tap restarts cleanly (no animation overlap)
- Theme not loaded yet (early boot): graceful degradation — push_warning + skip

**Estimated test count**: ~8 unit tests across 2 files

---

### S10-M4 scene-manager Story 009 (reduce_motion + offline-replay modal) — Integration

**Test file paths**:
- `tests/integration/scene_manager/reduce_motion_clamp_test.gd` (NEW)
- `tests/integration/scene_manager/offline_replay_modal_coordination_test.gd` (NEW)
- `tests/integration/scene_manager/reduce_motion_configfile_persist_test.gd` (NEW)

**What to test** (mapped to story-009 ACs):

- **`reduce_motion` clamp** (`reduce_motion_clamp_test.gd`):
  - With `reduce_motion = false`: CROSS_FADE duration = 150ms (default per ADR-0007)
  - With `reduce_motion = true`: CROSS_FADE duration clamped to ≤ 50ms (REDUCE_MOTION_CLAMP_MS)
  - SLIDE_*, FADE_TO_BLACK, PUSH_MODAL all clamp identically
  - CEREMONY transition becomes instant cut + reward number reveal (no AnimationPlayer.play call)
  - Touch feedback (per S10-M3) is NOT clamped (1.05× / 80ms preserved)
- **ConfigFile persist** (`reduce_motion_configfile_persist_test.gd`):
  - `set_reduce_motion(true)` writes `user://settings.cfg` with `[accessibility] reduce_motion = true`
  - SceneManager `_load_interim_settings()` on next boot reads the value back
  - Round-trip: set → save → reload → assert restored value
  - Missing file at boot: defaults to `false`, no error
- **Offline-replay modal coordination** (`offline_replay_modal_coordination_test.gd`):
  - `SceneManager.show_modal(modal_control)` adds modal to OverlayLayer with `pause_on_open = false`
  - On `OfflineProgressionEngine.offline_rewards_collected` signal emit: modal auto-dismisses via `hide_modal`
  - Modal does NOT pause the tick loop (offline replay needs ticks running for UI animations)
  - Threshold gating: silent for replays <100 ms estimated; modal shown for ≥100 ms (ADR-0014)

**Edge cases**:
- Toggle `reduce_motion` mid-transition: in-flight transition completes at original duration; next transition uses new value
- ConfigFile read error (corrupt file): defaults stand, push_warning logged
- Modal show called while another modal already open: behavior per Story 007 push_overlay (queue or reject — verify spec)
- `offline_rewards_collected` fires before modal even shown (sub-100ms replay): no-op, no crash

**Estimated test count**: ~10 integration tests across 3 files

---

### S10-S2 Story 014 — orchestrator state advancement during SceneManager TRANSITIONING — Integration

**Test file path**: `tests/integration/dungeon_run_orchestrator/state_advancement_during_transition_test.gd` (NEW)

**What to test**:

- Race-condition regression: orchestrator state transitions DURING SceneManager TRANSITIONING must be safe (the pattern that produced the S9-M2 fast-path regression)
- **Dual-path coverage check (gate-check Note 2 pattern)**: every test that exercises a screen's `_on_state_changed` slow path must have a sibling test that exercises the `on_enter` fast path with state already advanced
- Orchestrator state-machine validate_transition rejects invalid transitions during TRANSITIONING window
- Screen base class (or new wrapper) prevents listener subscription before SceneManager IDLE

**Edge cases**:
- RUN_ENDED fires DURING FADE_TO_BLACK into dungeon_run_view (the original S9-M2 scenario — already covered by `test_run_pacing_fast_path_dwell_holds_when_run_ended_at_on_enter`)
- DISPATCHING → ACTIVE_FOREGROUND fires DURING CROSS_FADE into formation_assignment
- Two state transitions back-to-back during TRANSITIONING: assert idempotency

**Estimated test count**: ~5 integration tests

---

### S10-S3 XP/level grant feedback — Logic + UI

**Test file path**: `tests/unit/dungeon_run_orchestrator/xp_grant_on_run_complete_test.gd` (NEW or extend existing)

**What to test** (depends on whether XP grant logic exists):

- **Logic side**: on RUN_ENDED with kill_count > 0, each hero in the formation receives XP per the formula (TBD — check hero-roster GDD §Formulas if it exists)
- Hero `xp` field increments by expected amount
- When `xp >= xp_threshold_for_level`: level increments; `xp` resets or carries over per spec
- `hero_leveled` signal emits with hero ID + new level

**Edge cases**:
- Zero kills: no XP granted (no signal emitted)
- Hero already at max level: XP granted but level capped (no overflow signal)
- Multiple heroes leveling in same run: signal emits once per hero, in formation order

**Estimated test count**: ~4 unit tests

### S10-N1 scene_manager test env flakes cleanup — Integration (test infra)

**Test file paths** (existing — to be fixed, not new):
- `tests/integration/scene_manager/modal_pause_tick_coupling_test.gd`
- `tests/integration/scene_manager/crossfade_timing_test.gd`
- `tests/integration/scene_manager/request_screen_and_node_swap_test.gd`

**What to fix**:
- Identify the Godot 4.6.1 mono headless wiring quirk causing intermittent failures
- Add `before_test` / `after_test` snapshot+restore where missing
- Verify each suite passes 10/10 consecutive runs in isolation AND in a full-suite pass

**Verification**: run `gdunit4_runner.gd` 10 times; assert all 3 suites pass each time.

### S10-N2 Cross-test autoload contamination cleanup — Integration (test infra)

**Test file path** (existing): `tests/unit/dungeon_run_orchestrator/autoload_skeleton_and_di_test.gd`

**What to fix**: 2 of 17 tests fail when run alongside other suites; pass 17/17 in isolation. Root cause: live-autoload state contamination from other tests.

**Fix**: add `before_test` snapshot of orchestrator state + `after_test` restore (mirrors the pattern used in `run_pacing_minimum_duration_test.gd`).

**Verification**: full suite run via `gdunit4_runner.gd`; assert 17/17 pass for this file.

### S10-N3 tr() safe-format helper — Logic

**Test file path**: `tests/unit/ui_framework/format_localized_test.gd` (NEW)

**What to test** (`UIFramework.format_localized(key, value)`):

- With format string containing `%s`: returns substituted string
- With format string lacking `%`: returns key passthrough + value appended
- Locale not loaded (key passthrough): returns key (does not crash)
- Edge: nil value → returns format string with empty substitution

**Estimated test count**: ~5 unit tests (one per branch + edge)

## Smoke Test Scope

Critical paths to verify before any QA hand-off for Sprint 10:

1. **Game launches to main menu without crash** (regression baseline)
2. **New game / fresh save can be started** (no `user://save_slot_1.dat` exists at launch)
3. **Full dispatch cycle works**: main_menu → formation_assignment → dungeon_run_view → run-end overlay → main_menu (regression baseline from Sprint 8/9)
4. **Save/reload cycle works** (S10-M1 BLOCKING): cold-launch with existing save restores Theron + roster + formation + last-floor state
5. **Heartbeat persist fires** (S10-M1 BLOCKING): leave game running for 60+ seconds; assert `user://save_slot_1.dat` mtime updates
6. **scene_boundary_persist fires** (S10-M1 BLOCKING): dispatch a run; assert save file updates BEFORE FADE_TO_BLACK into dungeon_run_view
7. **parchment_theme renders** (S10-M2 BLOCKING): all 4 production screens use parchment styling; no default Godot gray
8. **reduce_motion clamp works** (S10-M4 ADVISORY): toggle on; observe transitions ≤ 50ms
9. **Offline-replay modal appears** (S10-M4 ADVISORY): if computation ≥ 100ms, modal displays with parchment styling
10. **No frame spikes**: cold-launch + 60s idle + 3 dispatches; no frame drops below 60 fps target on PC
11. **No new errors**: console clean during full smoke walkthrough (push_warnings acceptable, push_errors are blockers)

*Smoke tests are verified by the developer via `/smoke-check sprint`. Reference this list when running that skill.*

---

## Playtest Requirements

| Story | Playtest Goal | Min Sessions | Target Player Type |
|---|---|---|---|
| S10-N5 Re-playtest with persisted save + offline replay | **Does the offline-game beat ("come back to find rewards") land?** This is the first playtest where the idle-game core fantasy is testable. Probe for verbatim core-fantasy quotes per gate-check Note 1 ("I felt like a guildmaster who left for a while and came back to..." or similar). | 1 (solo, post-S10-M1+M4) | Project lead simulating "deliberately new player who closed the game an hour ago" |

**Sign-off requirement**: Playtest notes must be written to `production/playtests/playtest-05-persisted-save-offline-replay-2026-XX-XX.md` and reviewed before S10-N5 can be marked COMPLETE. Mirror the structure of `playtest-04-post-sprint-9-polish-2026-05-05.md` for apples-to-apples comparison.

## Definition of Done — Sprint 10

A story is DONE when ALL of the following are true:

- [ ] All acceptance criteria verified — via automated test result OR documented manual evidence (screenshot, video, or playtest notes with sign-off)
- [ ] Test file exists at the specified path for all Logic and Integration stories (S10-M1, M3, M4, S2, S3, N1, N2, N3 — 8 stories)
- [ ] Manual evidence document exists for all Visual/Feel and UI stories (S10-M2, M3 UI portion, M4 UI portion, S3 UI portion, N4, N5 — 6 evidence docs)
- [ ] `/design-review` verdict for S10-S1 (Audio GDD) is not MAJOR REVISION NEEDED
- [ ] Smoke check passes (run `/smoke-check sprint` before QA hand-off)
- [ ] No regressions introduced (test suite ≥99% pass rate, expected to push toward 100% after S10-N1 + S10-N2)
- [ ] Code reviewed (via `/code-review` or documented peer review)
- [ ] Story file updated to `Status: Complete` (via `/story-done`)

---

## Process Notes (carried from 2026-05-05 gate-check)

Apply during test authoring AND code review:

1. **Dual-path coverage check (gate-check Note 2)** — for any code with a "fast path / slow path" structure (e.g., `on_enter` defensive branches + signal handlers, or `validate_transition` direct calls + queued path), ensure tests exercise BOTH paths. Pattern that produced the S9-M2 fast-path regression: dwell logic existed in slow path only; fast-path `call_deferred` shipped without dwell. **Apply specifically to S10-M1 (heartbeat path vs. scene-boundary path), S10-M4 (modal show via show_modal vs. push_overlay), S10-S2 (orchestrator state-machine surface)**.

2. **Verbatim core-fantasy capture (gate-check Note 1)** — S10-N5 playtest must explicitly probe for "did this feel like running a guild?"-type quotes. The Pillar 2 score is a proxy — collect direct fantasy-match statements when possible.

3. **GDD CONCERNS resolution (gate-check Note 4)** — as Audio GDD authoring (S10-S1) progresses, address relevant warnings flagged in `gdd-cross-review-2026-04-19.md` for audio integration with existing systems.
